title: $110 Million Tried to Pick Your Senator. The Biggest Check Lost.
title_alt: $110M of Hidden Money Hit the Texas Senate Race — and Backfired
The "Texans" Super PAC Spent $39M to Stop Paxton. He Won Anyway.
Who Really Paid? $110M Outside Money in the Texas Senate Race

title: They Spent $39 Million to Stop Him. He Won Anyway. #shorts
description:
An ad said "Texans for a Conservative Majority." The FEC filing said one man — Houston billionaire John Nau, $7.9M — bankrolling a pro-Cornyn super PAC that spent $39 million attacking Ken Paxton. Paxton won the May 26 primary anyway, ~64%. The biggest check in the race lost. Full receipt: campaignreceipts.com/articles/cr-tx-senate-2026-superpacs

AI DISCLOSURE: information-design cards + atmosphere visuals are AI-produced; all dollar figures and donor names are from primary FEC filings (committee C00542217).

#TexasSenate #SuperPAC #CampaignFinance #DarkMoney #FEC #Paxton #FactCheck #CampaignReceipts
_punchline: $39 million tried to stop one man. He won anyway. The biggest check in the race lost.
_qc: Stage 27 no-VO HARD gate PASS; Stage 25 visual QC PASS; punchline lands in-short
_note: published 2026-05-31, public, metadata+thumb pushed via update-meta

title: $110M Tried to Pick Your Senator. Your One Ballot Beat It. #shorts
description:
$110 million in outside money — the most expensive Senate race in the country — hit one Texas seat. The names sound like home: "Texans for a Conservative Majority," "Lone Star Rising." The checks came from out-of-state billionaires. You get one ballot. Full receipt: campaignreceipts.com/articles/cr-tx-senate-2026-superpacs

AI DISCLOSURE: information-design cards + atmosphere visuals are AI-produced; all dollar figures are from primary FEC filings.

#TexasSenate #SuperPAC #CampaignFinance #DarkMoney #FEC #FactCheck #CampaignReceipts
_punchline: $110 million tried to pick your senator. Your one ballot beat it.
